twitter_posts.title: This model is insane at design. I asked GLM 5.2 (left) and Opus 4.8 (right) to build me a landing page and you can't even tell the difference. GLM cost $0.06 while opus cost $0.49. More than 6x cheape
twitter_posts.description:
This model is insane at design.

I asked GLM 5.2 (left) and Opus 4.8 (right) to build me a landing page and you can't even tell the difference.

GLM cost $0.06 while opus cost $0.49. More than 6x cheaper while being faster + more token efficient.

Another win for open source AI.
twitter_posts.author: Hassan El Mghari

twitter_posts.title: R to @AnthropicAI: Watch the robodogs in action in our first Project Fetch experiment:
twitter_posts.description:
Watch the robodogs in action in our first Project Fetch experiment:


Anthropic (@AnthropicAI)

New Anthropic research: Project Fetch.

We asked two teams of Anthropic researchers to program a robot dog. Neither team had any robotics expertise—but we let only one team use Claude.
twitter_posts.author: Anthropic

twitter_posts.title: New Frontier Red Team blog: Phase 2 of Project Fetch, where we test how well Claude can program a robodog. Opus 4.7, on its own, was ~20x faster than last year's best human team aided by Opus 4.1. (Th
twitter_posts.description:
New Frontier Red Team blog: Phase 2 of Project Fetch, where we test how well Claude can program a robodog.

Opus 4.7, on its own, was ~20x faster than last year's best human team aided by Opus 4.1. (The robodog, alas, still failed to fetch a beach ball.)

articles.title: Pinned: Ran 10 more tests comparing GLM 5.2 & Opus. On average, GLM 5.2 produced 2x the tokens but was still faster + 3x cheaper with similar quality! I'm open sourcing all these tests tomorrow, inclu
articles.description:
Ran 10 more tests comparing GLM 5.2 & Opus.

On average, GLM 5.2 produced 2x the tokens but was still faster + 3x cheaper with similar quality!

I'm open sourcing all these tests tomorrow, including the code, my prompts, and the token/cost stats.

Video
  




Hassan (@nutlope)
